quick_facts.label: Program length
quick_facts.value: The VCI’s 2025–26 counselling bulletin describes B.V.Sc. & A.H. as a five-and-a-half-year degree that includes a compulsory one-year internship.

quick_facts.label: Primary entrance route
quick_facts.value: Admission is merit- and rank-based through NEET-UG, subject to the applicable eligibility criteria; the cited VCI bulletin states that no other merit or weightage is considered for B.V.Sc. & A.H. admission.

quick_facts.label: School subjects
quick_facts.value: For the VCI 15% All India Quota counselling in 2025–26, applicants needed 10+2/intermediate science with Physics, Chemistry, Biology or Biotechnology, and English as a core subject, alongside a qualified NEET-UG result.

quick_facts.label: National quota counselling
quick_facts.value: VCI conducted online counselling for 15% of B.V.Sc. & A.H. seats under its All India Quota in recognised government and private veterinary colleges for 2025–26; annual seat matrices, dates, and rules can change.

quick_facts.label: Professional registration
quick_facts.value: VCI’s direct-registration instructions require, among other documents, a degree/provisional degree certificate from a VCI-recognised university and a State Veterinary Council certificate.

sections.heading: Eligibility: science at 10+2 plus NEET-UG
paragraphs: B.V.Sc. & A.H. is entered after higher secondary education, not after a general bachelor’s degree. In the VCI’s 2025–26 All India Quota bulletin, the essential academic background was 10+2/intermediate science with Physics, Chemistry, Biology or Biotechnology, and English, together with a qualified NEET-UG result.

The same dated bulletin specified 50% aggregate in English, Physics, Chemistry, and Biology/Biotechnology for the General category, and 47.5% for SC, ST, or other special-category students as specified by government. It also set a minimum age of 17 years by December 31, 2025 for that counselling cycle. Candidates should treat category thresholds, disability provisions, nationality rules, age cut-offs, and document requirements as year-specific and confirm them in the applicable VCI/state counselling notice.
bullets: Choose the PCB/Biology pathway in Class 11–12 and retain English as a core subject.
Take and qualify NEET-UG for the relevant admission year.
Check the current VCI and state/university counselling bulletin before applying; do not rely on a prior year’s cut-offs or dates.

sections.heading: Duration and internship
paragraphs: The VCI’s 2025–26 counselling bulletin states that the B.V.Sc. & A.H. program lasts five and a half years and includes a compulsory one-year internship. The internship is therefore part of completing the degree rather than an optional add-on.

Institutional descriptions may use different academic-year or semester language, so applicants should verify the exact academic calendar and internship arrangement with the allotted college. The VCI bulletin is the more relevant reference for the national counselling framework.
bullets: Nominal duration: 5.5 years.
Includes a compulsory internship of one year.
Attendance, examinations, clinical work, and internship completion requirements are administered by the college/university under the applicable VCI standards.

sections.heading: What students study
paragraphs: B.V.Sc. & A.H. combines veterinary medicine with animal production and husbandry. The VCI lists the Minimum Standards of Veterinary Education—Degree Course (B.V.Sc. & A.H.) Regulations, 2016 among its governing education regulations, and university syllabi implementing these standards include veterinary anatomy, physiology, pathology, medicine, surgery and radiology, reproduction/gynaecology and obstetrics, as well as livestock-production-oriented study.

The program is not only classroom-based: training is intended to develop scientific, clinical, diagnostic, preventive-health, reproduction, surgery, medicine, and animal-management capability, followed by the compulsory internship described in the VCI counselling material.
bullets: Foundational biomedical areas: veterinary anatomy, physiology, biochemistry, microbiology, and pathology.
Clinical areas: veterinary medicine, surgery and radiology, and gynaecology/obstetrics.
Animal-science and husbandry areas: livestock production and management, nutrition, and genetics/breeding.
Applied learning: practical, clinical, field, and internship components.

sections.heading: How admission works
paragraphs: NEET-UG is the central examination route, but the counselling channel depends on the seat. For 2025–26, VCI’s online counselling covered 15% of seats under its All India Quota, with allotment based on NEET rank, candidate choices, seat availability, and verification of eligibility. The bulletin states that this quota covered recognised veterinary colleges, including government and private colleges.

For the VCI quota, the 2025–26 process used Round 1, Round 2, and a stray round. This is useful process context only: schedules, seat matrices, counselling fees, participating colleges, and reservation implementation must be checked for the target academic year. State-level and institutional processes can apply outside the VCI quota.
bullets: Write NEET-UG and retain the scorecard/rank details.
Follow the current VCI portal for the 15% All India Quota, where applicable.
Follow the relevant state or university admission authority for seats outside that quota.
Before locking choices, verify college recognition, tuition and other charges, hostel arrangements, and reporting requirements.

sections.heading: Costs: college-specific, not nationally fixed
paragraphs: There is no single national tuition figure for B.V.Sc. & A.H. that applicants can safely use. VCI’s 2025–26 bulletin explicitly states that private-college fees are decided by the relevant state government or university/college, and that academic-session charges, tuition, and other fees may vary by college, university, and state. VCI advises candidates to confirm charges before exercising choices.

When comparing offers, separate tuition from one-time admission/caution charges, hostel and mess costs, examination fees, clinical or laboratory charges, and any counselling fees. Use the fee notice issued by the specific college or state authority for the intended admission year.
bullets: Do not infer a college’s total cost from its NEET closing rank or ownership alone.
Obtain the current, written fee schedule from the college/university before accepting an allotment.
Treat historical fees as dated context rather than a future commitment.

sections.heading: After B.V.Sc. & A.H.: practice, postgraduate study, research, and public-service pathways
paragraphs: A common academic progression is M.V.Sc., followed by doctoral study or research. Birsa Agricultural University identifies M.V.Sc. and Ph.D. as postgraduate options and states that B.V.Sc. & A.H. graduates with the required GPA/OGPA may be admitted under university regulations. In 2026, NTA’s notices include ICAR AIEEA (PG), so prospective postgraduate applicants should consult that year’s information bulletin and the chosen university’s subject eligibility rules rather than assuming a uniform route.

For veterinary practice, registration requirements matter. VCI’s direct-registration page requires a recognised-university degree/provisional degree certificate and a State Veterinary Council certificate among its documents. Employment routes—such as veterinary officer posts, animal-health technical services, clinics, research projects, teaching, livestock-sector roles, or entrepreneurship—are vacancy- and employer-specific and may impose separate registration, examination, experience, domicile, or reservation conditions.
bullets: Postgraduate study: M.V.Sc.; thereafter Ph.D. or research-focused training.
Entrance context: ICAR AIEEA (PG) is an active 2026 examination route; verify discipline eligibility and participating institutions in the current bulletin.
Professional pathway: complete the degree and meet State Veterinary Council/VCI registration requirements as applicable.
Employment pathway: monitor official state animal-husbandry departments, universities, research institutes, and public recruitment bodies for post-specific eligibility.
